mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth directly boosts corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This effect persists regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 9.0% signals a tightening labor market that reduces wage-driven inflation fears and supports consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-stat < 1.65) when UNRATE drops below 9.0% over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which lifts equity valuations over a 60-day horizon through improved cash flow projections.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates compress equity discount rates and signal policy tightening, which reduces forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of months where FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects stronger corporate capacity utilization and higher output, which translates directly into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly INDPRO changes and 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, it confirms economic acceleration that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 2.5% annualized
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in > 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent job creation and unemployment decline indicate a self-reinforcing labor market recovery, which sustains positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon through stable consumer demand.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE does not yield a positive predictive Rank IC for 60-day SPX returns over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid increases in housing starts outpacing industrial production growth signal demand overheating that triggers supply bottlenecks and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where HOUST growth exceeds INDPRO growth by > 1.0 percentage point.
